name: FGI Industries Ltd.
business: FGI is a global, diversified and reputable supplier of quality bath and kitchen products. Our products are typically designed in-house or are created in conjunction with our customer and supplier partners. The majority of our products are sold under our customers private label brands.
namedSuppliers: Tangshan Huida Ceramic Group Co., Ltd (Huida)

exposures.sentence: Our main owned brands in this category include Foremost, which is retail-focused, and contrac, which is wholesale-focused.
We sell a growing number of our products through the e-commerce channels of our retail partners as well as e-commerce only retailers such as Build.com and Wayfair.com.

The primary drivers of such consistent and above-GDP growth rates are the pace of household formation, home price appreciation, strong housing turnover and the continued aging of the U.S. housing stock in our primary geographic markets.
While our sales are principally impacted by the growth of the R&R markets, we are selectively focusing on newbuild markets as well.

catalysts.sentence: On July 28, 2025, the Company filed an amendment ... to effect a 1-for-5 reverse share split ... which was effected on July 31, 2025.

catalysts.event: Renewal of East West Bank credit facility
catalysts.sentence: On March 27, 2026, FGI Industries renewed its credit facility with East West Bank, extending the maturity to April 17, 2027.
Termination of the lease for one of its warehouse facilities in the first quarter of 2025, which resulted in a non-recurring lease exit cost.

catalysts.event: Entry into 5-year licensing agreement for overflow toilet technology
catalysts.sentence: In 2025, we entered into a 5-year licensing agreement that will provide us access to an industry leading overflow toilet technology.

tamClaims.claim: Projected consumer spend for U.S. bath and kitchen markets is approximately $228 billion in 2026, with about half in product categories FGI operates within.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Kitchen and Bath Association, the projected consumer spend for the U.S. bath and kitchen markets is estimated to be approximately $228 billion in 2026, of which approximately half is in product categories that we currently operate within.
